name: Pico

unions.name: Layout
unions.comment: Used to define the layout of the application

aliases.name: Cell
aliases.comment:
A (table) cell can contain multiple Html elements.
    This is just an alias to make the type signature of
    functions that can receive it more readable.

aliases.name: Column
aliases.comment:
A grid column can contain multiple cells that
    can contain multiple Html elements.
    This is just an alias to make the type signature of
    functions that can receive `Columns` more readable.

aliases.name: Row
aliases.comment:
A (table) row can contain multiple cells that 
    can contain multiple Html elements.
    This is just an alias to make the type signature of
    functions that can receive it more readable.

values.name: abbr
values.comment:
Abbreviation element
The first argument is the abbreviation and the second is the meaning.

    abbr "tldr" "too long didn't  read"

values.name: accordion
values.comment:
Renders an [accordion](https://picocss.com/docs/accordion).

    accordion [ asButton ] [ text "open me" ] [ text "the glorious details" ]
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: asButton
values.comment:
Give an element (for example a link) the look of a button
a [asButton][text "Click me!"]

values.name: contrastButton
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: contrastLink
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: dropdown
values.comment:
Pico uses the [details element](https://picocss.com/docs/dropdown) as markdown for a dropdown.
This function lets you create a dropdown from the "collapsed" content and a multiple items to display in the expanded state.
values.type: Pico.Cell msg -> Pico.Column msg -> Html.Html msg

values.name: errorView
values.comment: Displays errors next to an input field.
values.type: (FancyForms.FormState.Error error -> String.String) -> List.List (FancyForms.FormState.Error error) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg)

values.name: grid
values.comment:
Creates a [simple responseive grid](https://picocss.com/docs/grid)
with the given class name.

values.name: includeFromCDN
values.comment: Include the CSS for the given theme.

values.name: ltr
values.comment: A shortcut for dir="ltr" to set the text direction to left-to-right.

values.name: main_
values.type: Pico.Layout -> Pico.Theme.ColorScheme -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: nav
values.comment:
A helper to construct a `nav` element in a more concise way.
    In [Pico.css](https://picocss.com/docs/nav) the `nav` element
    is laid out as a list of columns.

values.name: outline
values.comment: Render this button in the "outline" style

values.name: primaryButton
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: primaryLink
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: rtl
values.comment: A shortcut for dir="rtl" to set the text direction to right-to-left.

values.name: secondaryButton
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: secondaryLink
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: switch
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: table
values.comment:
A helper to construct a table in a more concise way.
  It takes the attributes for the table, the header row and
  the list of rows.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> Pico.Row msg -> List.List (Pico.Row msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: tooltip
values.comment: Adds a tooltip displaying on top of the element.

values.name: tooltipBottom
values.comment: Adds a tooltip displaying at the bottom of the element.

values.name: tooltipLeft
values.comment: Adds a tooltip displaying to the left of the element.

values.name: tooltipRight
values.comment: Adds a tooltip displaying to the right of the element.

name: Pico.Form
comment:
This module provides [FancyForm](https://blog.axelerator.de/fancy-forms/) widgets that allow
you to build forms that use the markup of the [pico](https://picocss.com) CSS framework.


## Widgets

@docs textInput, dropdown, integerInput, floatInput, dateInput, switch

values.name: dateInput
values.comment: A `Date` input with the given label

values.name: dropdown
values.comment: A `Select` widget with the given label
values.type: String.String -> FancyForms.Form.Variants b -> FancyForms.Widgets.Dropdown.SelectWidget b customError

values.name: floatInput
values.comment: A `Float` input with the given labelText and attributes

values.name: integerInput
values.comment: An `Integer` input with the given label

values.name: textInput
values.comment: A `String` input with the given label

name: Pico.Modal
comment:
# Modal component

Uses the [styles from Pico](https://picocss.com/docs/modal) to display a modal.
The component comes with pretty animations and the functionality to "lock" scrolling
when the modal is open.

This does however require interaction with the page outside the modal.
You need to include the webcomponent that's in `picomponent.js` in the packages repository root.


## Setup

**There must be only one instance of the modal component.**
since there can never be multiple modals open at the same time.

For the animation the component needs it's own state.
So it is implemented with the Elm architecture.

You need to:

1.  add a `ModalState` field to your model
2.  initialize it with `Modal.init`
3.  add a variant to your `Mgs` type that takes a `Modal.Msg` as an argument
4.  forward the `Modal.Msg` to `Modal.update` and update your `ModalState` accordingly
5.  subscribe to `Modal.subscription` (and tell it which of your `Msg` to use to wrap the `Modal.Msg`)
6.  use `Modal.view` to render your modal

@docs ModalState, init, Msg, update, subscription, view


## Control visibility

@docs open, close, toggle

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: The messages that will be sent to control the state of the modal.

values.name: close
values.comment:
Closes the modal. Takes the current state of the modal to avoid
closing and already closed modal.

values.name: init
values.comment: The initial, closed state of the modal.

values.name: open
values.comment:
Opens the modal. Takes the current state of the modal to avoid
opening and already opened modal.

values.name: subscription
values.comment: Listens to the escape key to close the modal.

name: Pico.Theme

unions.name: ColorScheme
unions.comment: Pico CSS comes with both Light and Dark color schemes, automatically enabled based on user preferences.
